cases.prompt: A Next.js app has output: 'export' in its config. A ticket asks you to add an API route handler that reads request cookies to personalize a page. What do you do?
Your change works perfectly on the Next.js dev server. List the verification chain you run before handing off, given the deploy artifact is the static out/ directory.

You need the app to know a backend endpoint URL at runtime in a Next.js static export. A teammate suggests reading process.env.BACKEND_URL in the component. Explain what actually happens with env vars in a static export and what the correct pattern is.
A statically exported app has a dynamic route /items/[id]. New items are added to the database weekly. Why is this a problem for the export, and what are the standard resolutions?
You just modified the currency conversion and checkout total logic in a static-export storefront. What extra gate applies to this change before it merges, beyond the normal verification chain?

The repo uses a framework version released after your training data, and its node_modules or docs/ folder ships the framework's own documentation. You're unsure whether an API you remember still exists. What do you do?
The same static-export app also ships as an Android APK via Capacitor. After changing frontend code, what does the mobile variant additionally require before you can claim the change works on mobile?
